title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school has asked you to write a report on a survey carried out among students regarding their attitudes towards animal welfare and pet ownership. Include findings about students' current attitudes, describe any concerns raised, and make recommendations for how the school could raise awareness of animal welfare issues.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Attitudes to Animal Welfare at Hollybrook School

Introduction
This report presents findings from a survey completed by 185 students on the topics of animal welfare, pet ownership, and related ethical issues.

Current Attitudes
The results revealed broadly positive attitudes towards animal welfare, with 79% of respondents stating that they considered animal suffering an important moral issue. However, awareness of specific welfare concerns — such as factory farming conditions or the exotic pet trade — was significantly lower, with fewer than a third of students able to identify the main issues involved.

Key Concerns Raised
Several students expressed concern about the welfare standards of animals kept in local pet shops, while others noted that they had little reliable information about how to care properly for the pets they owned. A minority admitted to purchasing pets from unverified online sources.

Recommendations
The school should introduce a dedicated animal welfare session as part of the science or PSHE curriculum. Inviting a representative from a local animal rescue charity to speak to students would also be highly effective. Additionally, displaying informative posters throughout the school could raise everyday awareness.

Conclusion
Students are willing to engage with animal welfare issues but require better access to accurate information.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Are zoos still relevant in the modern world? Some people argue that zoos are cruel and outdated, while others insist they play a vital role in conservation. We want to hear your opinion — write an article sharing your views.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Behind Bars: Do Zoos Still Deserve Our Support?

The debate surrounding zoos has never been more heated. In an era of growing environmental awareness, many people are questioning whether confining wild animals for public entertainment can ever truly be justified — regardless of the educational benefits claimed.

Those who defend zoos point, with some justification, to their conservation programmes. Several species — including the Arabian oryx and the California condor — owe their survival partly to captive breeding initiatives run by accredited zoos. Undeniably, these institutions also educate millions of visitors each year about biodiversity and the threats facing wildlife.

However, critics rightly argue that the welfare of individual animals must not be sacrificed for the sake of conservation goals. Large, intelligent animals such as elephants and great apes frequently display signs of psychological distress in captivity.

I believe the future lies in transforming traditional zoos into genuine wildlife sanctuaries — places focused on rehabilitation and release rather than permanent exhibition. If zoos are willing to evolve, they can still play a meaningful role. If not, perhaps it is time to reconsider their place in our world.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Jamie is thinking about getting a pet and has been looking at puppies for sale online from unknown breeders. Write an email to Jamie encouraging them to adopt a rescue pet instead, explaining the benefits of adoption, describing any concerns about buying from unknown sources, and suggesting practical next steps.

Write your email to Jamie.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Jamie,

I am so glad you are thinking about getting a pet — it is honestly one of the best decisions I have ever made! But before you buy from those online listings, I really want to encourage you to consider adoption first.

Rescue centres are absolutely full of wonderful animals of all ages and breeds who desperately need loving homes. When you adopt, the centre staff can tell you in detail about the animal's personality, health history, and needs — something unknown online sellers rarely provide.

I have to be honest: buying from unverified breeders online carries real risks. Many are operating irresponsibly or even illegally, and the animals are often kept in poor conditions. You could end up with a pet that has serious health or behavioural issues, and without any comeback.

My suggestion would be to visit your nearest rescue centre this weekend — just to look! I guarantee you will fall in love.

Adoption fees are usually much lower than buying prices, and the animals have typically been vaccinated and neutered already.

Let me know if you want company!

Cheers,
Sophie
